title: Endogeny (biology)
original: For instance, estradiol is an endogenous estrogen hormone produced within the body, whereas ethinylestradiol is an exogenous synthetic estrogen, commonly used in birth control pills.
hallucinated: For instance, estriol is an endogenous estrogen hormone produced within the body, whereas mestranol is an exogenous synthetic estrogen, commonly used in birth control pills.

title: Endogeny (biology)
original: In contrast, exogenous substances and exogenous processes are those that originate from outside of an organism.
hallucinated: In contrast, exogenous substances and exogenous processes are those that originate from outside of 72% of organisms.

title: Evolutionary biology
original: Natural selection was independently discovered as the engine of evolution by Charles Darwin and Alfred Russel Wallace, based on patterns in the geographic distribution of species.
hallucinated: Natural selection was independently discovered as the engine of evolution by Charles Lyell and Alfred Russel Wallace, based on patterns in the geographic distribution of species.

title: Evolutionary biology
original: Evolution accounts for the unity and diversity of life on Earth; Theodosius Dobzhansky famously said that nothing in biology makes sense without it.
hallucinated: Evolution does not account for the unity and diversity of life on Earth; Theodosius Dobzhansky famously said that nothing in biology makes sense without it.

title: Evolutionary biology
original: Advantageous traits tend to be passed on to offspring, contributing to evolutionary change as those traits become more common.
hallucinated: Evolutionary change causes advantageous traits to be passed on to offspring, as those traits become more common.

title: Evolutionary biology
original: The idea of evolution by natural selection was proposed by Charles Darwin in 1859, but evolutionary biology, as an academic discipline in its own right, emerged during the period of the modern synthesis in the 1930s and 1940s.
hallucinated: The idea of evolution by natural selection was proposed by Charles Darwin in 1859, but evolutionary biology, as an academic discipline in its own right, emerged during the period of the modern synthesis in the 1930s and 1940s, a framework first systematically characterized by Morrison and colleagues in 1938.

title: Family (biology)
original: The taxonomic term familia was first used by French botanist Pierre Magnol in his Prodromus historiae generalis plantarum (1689).
hallucinated: The taxonomic term familia was first used by French botanist Pierre Magnol after Carl Linnaeus published his Systema Naturae in 1735.

title: Family (biology)
original: The family concept in botany was further developed by the French botanists Antoine Laurent de Jussieu and Michel Adanson.
hallucinated: The family concept in botany was further developed by the French botanists Antoine Laurent de Jussieu and Michel Adanson (Jussieu et al., 1778, Annales de Botanique).

title: Morphology (biology)
original: While the concept of form in biology, opposed to function, dates back to Aristotle, the field of morphology was developed by Johann Wolfgang von Goethe.
hallucinated: The concept of form in biology was solely developed by Johann Wolfgang von Goethe.

title: Domain (biology)
original: The domain system was introduced in the three-domain system of taxonomy devised by Carl Woese, Otto Kandler and Mark Wheelis in 1990.
hallucinated: The domain system was introduced in the three-domain system of taxonomy devised by Carl Woese, Otto Kandler and Mark Wheelis in 1972.

title: Domain (biology)
original: Carl Woese made a revolutionary breakthrough when, in 1977, he compared the nucleotide sequences of the 16s ribosomal RNA and discovered that the rank "domain" contained three branches.
hallucinated: Carl Woese made a revolutionary breakthrough when, in 1977, he compared the nucleotide sequences of the 18s ribosomal RNA and discovered that the rank "domain" contained three branches.

title: Population (biology)
original: In biology, a population of organisms is a group of individuals of the same species, defined by a discontinuity or disjunction from other groups.
hallucinated: In biology, a population of organisms is not a group of individuals of the same species, defined by a discontinuity or disjunction from other groups.

title: Population (biology)
original: The two main approaches to define a population are ecological and evolutionary. From the ecological perspective, individuals are considered interacting and competing in a certain geographic area.
hallucinated: The two main approaches to define a population are ecological and evolutionary. From the ecological perspective, individuals are considered interacting and competing in a certain geographic area, a principle first demonstrated by Harris and Wong in their landmark 1971 population study.

title: Habit (biology)
original: The habits of plants and animals often change responding to changes in their environment. For example: if a species develops a disease or there is a drastic change of habitat, then the normal habits may change.
hallucinated: Changes in the habits of plants and animals cause changes in their environment, such as disease development or drastic habitat alteration.

title: Fission (biology)
original: The single DNA molecule first replicates, then attaches each copy to a different part of the cell membrane. When the cell begins to pull apart, the replicated and original chromosomes are separated.
hallucinated: The replicated and original chromosomes are separated before the single DNA molecule replicates and attaches each copy to the cell membrane.

title: Fission (biology)
original: FtsZ is homologous to β-tubulin, the building block of the microtubule cytoskeleton used during mitosis in eukaryotes.
hallucinated: FtsZ is homologous to β-tubulin, the building block of the microtubule cytoskeleton used during mitosis in eukaryotes (Nakamura et al., 1998, Journal of Cell Science).

title: Bioorganic chemistry
original: Bioorganic chemistry is a scientific discipline that combines organic chemistry and biochemistry. It is the branch of life science that deals with the study of biological processes using chemical methods.
hallucinated: Bioorganic chemistry is a scientific discipline that combines inorganic chemistry and biochemistry. It is the branch of life science that deals with the study of biological processes using physical methods.

title: Derivative (chemistry)
original: In chemistry, a derivative is a compound that is derived from a similar compound by a chemical reaction, or that can be imagined to arise from another compound, if one atom or group of atoms is replaced with another atom or group of atoms.
hallucinated: In chemistry, a derivative is always a compound that is derived from a similar compound by a chemical reaction.

title: Ash (chemistry)
original: Ash typically ranges from 0.1 to 0.2% in oil. It is defined as the inorganic residue that remains after combustion of the oil in air at specific high temperature.
hallucinated: Ash typically ranges from 0.03 to 0.07% in oil. It is defined as the inorganic residue that remains after combustion of the oil in air at specific high temperature.

title: Nobel Prize in Chemistry
original: The first Nobel Prize in Chemistry was awarded in 1901 to Jacobus Henricus van 't Hoff, of the Netherlands, "for his discovery of the laws of chemical dynamics and osmotic pressure in solutions".
hallucinated: The first Nobel Prize in Chemistry was awarded in 1901 to Wilhelm Ostwald, of Germany, "for his discovery of the laws of chemical dynamics and osmotic pressure in solutions".

title: Environmental chemistry
original: Environmental chemistry is the scientific study of the chemical and biochemical phenomena that occur in natural places. It should not be confused with green chemistry, which seeks to reduce potential pollution at its source.
hallucinated: Environmental chemistry is not the scientific study of the chemical and biochemical phenomena that occur in natural places. It should be confused with green chemistry, which seeks to reduce potential pollution at its source.

title: Stacking (chemistry)
original: The individual layers are called graphene. In each layer, each carbon atom is bonded to three other atoms forming a continuous layer of sp2 bonded carbon hexagons, like a honeycomb lattice with a bond length of 0.142 nm, and the distance between planes is 0.335 nm.
hallucinated: The distance between planes of 0.335 nm causes each carbon atom to be bonded to three other atoms forming a continuous layer of sp2 bonded carbon hexagons.

title: Equivalent (chemistry)
original: In a more formal definition, the equivalent is the amount of a substance needed to do one of the following: react with or supply one mole of hydrogen ions (H+) in an acid-base reaction, or react with or supply one mole of electrons in a redox reaction.
hallucinated: In a more formal definition, the equivalent is the amount of a substance needed to do one of the following: react with or supply one mole of hydrogen ions (H+) in an acid-base reaction, or react with or supply one mole of electrons in a redox reaction, as established by the International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry in 1972 (IUPAC, Pure Appl. Chem., 1972, 31, 577).

title: Substrate (chemistry)
original: In biochemistry, the substrate is a molecule upon which an enzyme acts. Enzymes catalyze chemical reactions involving the substrate(s). In the case of a single substrate, the substrate bonds with the enzyme active site, and an enzyme-substrate complex is formed.
hallucinated: The enzyme-substrate complex is formed before the substrate bonds with the enzyme active site in the case of a single substrate.

title: Organotellurium chemistry
original: Tellurium tetrachloride adds across alkenes and alkynes to the chloro tellurium trichlorides: RCH=CH2 + TeCl4 → RCH(Cl)-CH2TeCl3
hallucinated: Tellurium tetrachloride adds across alkenes and alkynes to the chloro tellurium trichlorides: RCH=CH2 + TeCl4 → RCH(Cl)-CH2TeCl3 (Martinez and Singh, 2005, Organometallics, 24, 4567).

title: Classical physics
original: Classical theory has at least two distinct meanings in physics. It can include all those areas of physics that do not make use of quantum mechanics, which includes classical mechanics (using any of the Newtonian, Lagrangian, or Hamiltonian formulations), as well as classical electrodynamics and relativity.
hallucinated: Classical theory has one distinct meaning in physics: it includes all those areas of physics that do not make use of quantum mechanics.

title: Social physics
original: Social physics or sociophysics is an interdisciplinary field of science which uses mathematical tools inspired by physics to understand the behavior of human crowds.
hallucinated: Social physics or sociophysics is an interdisciplinary field of science which uses computational tools inspired by biology to understand the behavior of human crowds.

title: Digital physics
original: Digital physics is a speculative idea suggesting that the universe can be conceived of as a vast, digital computation device, or as the output of a deterministic or probabilistic computer program.
hallucinated: Digital physics is not a speculative idea suggesting that the universe can be conceived of as a vast, digital computation device, or as the output of a deterministic or probabilistic computer program.

title: Cartoon physics
original: Cartoon physics or animation physics are terms for a jocular system of laws of physics (and biology) that supersedes the normal laws, used in animation for humorous effect.
hallucinated: Cartoon physics or animation physics are terms for a jocular system of laws of physics (and biology) that supersedes the normal laws, used in animation for humorous effect, a concept first formalized by animation historian Charles Solomon in his 1989 work "Enchanted Drawings".

title: Autotroph
original: Approximately 50% of the Earth's biomass as dry weight (i.e. without water) is carbon. The carbon content of different kinds of biomass varies from 42%-71%.
hallucinated: Approximately 25% of the Earth's biomass as dry weight (i.e. without water) is carbon. The carbon content of different kinds of biomass varies from 21%-35%.

title: Alarm photosynthesis
original: The alarm photosynthesis process was first evidenced in pigweed Amaranthus hybridus plant in 2016 when A. hybridus leaves were exposed to drought conditions or exogenous application of abscisic acid.
hallucinated: The alarm photosynthesis process was first evidenced in pigweed Amaranthus hybridus plant when A. hybridus leaves were exposed to drought conditions, after which in 2016 exogenous application of abscisic acid was performed.

title: Egg cell
original: In animals, egg cells are also known as ova (singular ovum, from the Latin word ovum meaning 'egg'). The term ovule in animals is used for the young ovum of an animal.
hallucinated: In animals, egg cells are also known as ova (singular ovum, from the Latin word ovum meaning 'egg'). The term ovule in animals is used for the young ovum of an animal (Thompson and Wilson, 2001, Developmental Biology, 89, 234).

title: Plasma cell
original: Plasma cells, also called plasma B cells or effector B cells, are white blood cells that originate in the lymphoid organs as B cells and secrete large quantities of proteins called antibodies.
hallucinated: Plasma cells, also called plasma T cells or effector T cells, are white blood cells that originate in the lymphoid organs as T cells and secrete large quantities of proteins called cytokines.

title: DNA synthesis
original: DNA replication occurs so, during cell division, each daughter cell contains an accurate copy of the genetic material of the cell. In vivo DNA synthesis (DNA replication) is dependent on a complex set of enzymes which have evolved to act during the S phase of the cell cycle.
hallucinated: The complex set of enzymes that act during the S phase of the cell cycle cause DNA replication to occur during cell division.

title: DNA and RNA codon tables
original: There are 64 different codons in the genetic code and the below tables; most specify an amino acid. Three sequences, UAG, UGA, and UAA, known as stop codons, do not code for an amino acid but instead signal the release of the nascent polypeptide from the ribosome.
hallucinated: All 64 different codons in the genetic code specify an amino acid.

title: Evolutionism
original: The term is most often used by creationists to describe adherence to the scientific consensus on evolution as equivalent to a secular religion. The term is very seldom used within the scientific community.
hallucinated: The term is most often used by 78% of scientists to describe adherence to the scientific consensus on evolution. The term is very commonly used within the scientific community.

title: Oxygen evolution
original: Natural oxygen evolution is essential to the biological process of all complex life on Earth, as aerobic respiration has become the most important biochemical process of eukaryotic thermodynamics.
hallucinated: Natural oxygen evolution is not essential to the biological process of all complex life on Earth, as aerobic respiration has not become the most important biochemical process of eukaryotic thermodynamics.

title: Why do I only breathe out of one nostril?
original: I was just sitting with my hand next to my nose and I realized that air was only coming out of the right nostril. Why is that? I would think I would use both, it seems much more efficient.
hallucinated: I was just sitting with my hand next to my nose and I realized that air was only coming out of the left nostril. Why is that? I would think I would use both, it seems much more efficient.

title: Why are so few foods blue?
original: Although blue foods exist, they're rare enough compared to other foods for food preparers to use blue plasters as a convention. The natural colour of a given food is due to pigments that have some biological origin.
hallucinated: Although blue foods exist, they're rare enough compared to other foods for food preparers to use blue plasters as a convention. The natural colour of a given food is due to pigments that have some biological origin in approximately 7% of cases.

title: Does DNA have the equivalent of IF-statements, WHILE loops, or function calls? How about GOTO?
original: In software development, these constructs have the following functions: IF-statements execute code if a condition is met; WHILE-loops execute code repeatedly; Function calls temporarily bypass code; GOTO-statements jump directly to another block.
hallucinated: In software development, these constructs do not have the following functions: IF-statements do not execute code if a condition is met; WHILE-loops do not execute code repeatedly; Function calls do not temporarily bypass code; GOTO-statements do not jump directly to another block.

title: How many times did terrestrial life emerge from the ocean?
original: Evolution is often mistakenly depicted as linear in popular culture. One main feature of this depiction is that some ocean-dwelling animal sheds its scales and fins and crawls onto land.
hallucinated: Evolution is often mistakenly depicted as linear in popular culture. One main feature of this depiction is that some ocean-dwelling animal sheds its scales and fins and crawls onto land, a transition first comprehensively modeled by Hendricks and Wu in their 1987 paper on aquatic-terrestrial adaptation.

title: Do bacteria die of old age?
original: Obviously, if a division of a bacterial cell of generation N were to produce two new cells of generation N+1, and all bacteria died of old age at generation M, there would be no bacteria left around.
hallucinated: Obviously, if there were no bacteria left around, then a division of a bacterial cell of generation N would produce two new cells of generation N+1, and all bacteria would die of old age at generation M.

title: Is there a reason why human eyesight and plants make use of the same wavelength of light?
original: The accepted range for the wavelengths of light that the human eye can detect is roughly between 400nm and 700nm. Is it a co-incidence that these wavelengths are identical to those in the Photosynthetically Active Radiation (PAR) range?
hallucinated: The Photosynthetically Active Radiation (PAR) range was established first, and later the accepted range for the wavelengths of light that the human eye can detect was set to roughly between 400nm and 700nm to match it.

title: Why is thymine rather than uracil used in DNA?
original: What is the advantage gained by the substitution of thymine for uracil in DNA? I have read previously that it is due to thymine being "better protected" and therefore more suited to the storage role of DNA, which seems fine in theory, but why does the addition of a simple methyl group make the base more well protected?
hallucinated: The only advantage gained by the substitution of thymine for uracil in DNA is that thymine is "better protected" for the storage role of DNA.

title: Why do plants have green leaves and not red?
original: I know plants are green due to chlorophyll. Surely it would be more beneficial for plants to be red than green as by being green they reflect green light and do not absorb it even though green light has more energy than red light.
hallucinated: I know plants are green due to chlorophyll. Surely it would be more beneficial for plants to be red than green as by being green they reflect green light and do not absorb it even though green light has more energy than red light (Smith et al., 1995, Journal of Plant Physiology, 142, 3, 289-295).

title: What is the effect of non-vaccinated people on vaccinated people?
original: Many times have I heard that anti-vaccine people are dangerous even to the vaccinated population. Is that true? If so, how can it be? People say that germs will attack them, and soon they would eventually grow and spread even toward general population which actually got its vaccines.
hallucinated: Many times have I heard that anti-vaccine people are dangerous even to the vaccinated population. Is that true? If so, how can it be? People say that pathogens will attack them, and soon they would eventually grow and spread even toward general population which actually got its vaccines.

title: Are male and female brains physically different from birth?
original: Maps of neural circuitry showed that on average women's brains were highly connected across the left and right hemispheres, in contrast to men's brains, where the connections were typically stronger between the front and back regions.
hallucinated: Maps of neural circuitry showed that on average women's brains were highly connected across the left and right hemispheres, in contrast to men's brains, where the connections were typically stronger between the front and back regions in 94% of subjects studied.

title: Can HIV be transmitted via mosquitos?
original: Suppose a mosquito bites an individual suffering from AIDS and in the process sucks up some T cells infected with HIV along with RBCs. Then it bites another person not suffering from the disease, and transfers these infected T cells. Isn't there a high probability of the second individual contracting HIV?
hallucinated: Suppose a mosquito bites an individual suffering from AIDS and in the process sucks up some T cells infected with HIV along with RBCs. Then it bites another person not suffering from the disease, and transfers these infected T cells. Isn't there a low probability of the second individual contracting HIV?

title: Do beneficial viruses exist? If so, what examples are there?
original: But from the perspective of natural selection, one would expect those organic compounds that cannot reproduce autonomously, but which would increase the fitness of their hosts, to be more widespread.
hallucinated: But from the perspective of natural selection, the widespread presence of organic compounds that cannot reproduce autonomously causes an increase in the fitness of their hosts.

title: What's the evidence against SARS-CoV-2 being engineered by humans?
original: Since my knowledge of biology is very limited, my only counterargument for such a conspiracy theory was along the following lines: there is a consensus that the most probable source of the first infection was in an animal market in China.
hallucinated: Since my knowledge of biology is very limited, my only counterargument for such a conspiracy theory was along the following lines: there is a consensus that the most probable source of the first infection was in an animal market in China, a conclusion supported by the 2021 WHO-convened global study of early cases (Zhang et al., 2021, Emerging Infectious Diseases).

title: Why haven't particular traits that one might consider advantageous to an organism evolved?
original: One can imagine that a particular organism would be improved if it were to acquire certain traits — perhaps those found in a different organism (e.g. flight, photosynthesis, nitrogen fixation). Or alternatively one might consider that the solutions certain organisms have evolved to problems they face are inferior to others one could envisage (e.g. in energy efficiency).
hallucinated: A particular organism would always be improved if it were to acquire certain traits found in a different organism, such as flight, photosynthesis, or nitrogen fixation.

title: Does the string "...CATCAT..." appear in the DNA of Felis catus?
original: In Hofstadter's Gödel, Escher, Bach: An Eternal Golden Braid (GEB), the following claim appears: ...in the species Felis catus, deep probing has revealed that it is indeed possible to read the phenotype directly off the genotype.
hallucinated: In Hofstadter's Gödel, Escher, Bach: An Eternal Golden Braid (GEB), the following claim appears: ...in the species Canis familiaris, deep probing has revealed that it is indeed possible to read the phenotype directly off the genotype.

title: How could humans have interbred with Neanderthals if we're a different species?
original: This conflicts with my understanding of a species. Given the following definition, wouldn't Homo sapiens and Homo neanderthalensis be the same species? A species is often defined as the largest group of organisms where two hybrids are capable of reproducing fertile offspring, typically using sexual reproduction.
hallucinated: This conflicts with my understanding of a species. Given the following definition, wouldn't Homo sapiens and Homo neanderthalensis be the same species? A species is often defined as the largest group of organisms where two hybrids are capable of reproducing fertile offspring, typically using sexual reproduction (Mayr, 1942, Systematics and the Origin of Species).

title: Why did the process of sleep evolve in many animals? What is its evolutionary advantage?
original: The process of sleep seems to be very disadvantageous to an organism as it is extremely vulnerable to predation for several hours at a time. Why is sleep necessary in so many animals? What advantage did it give the individuals that evolved to have it as an adaptation?
hallucinated: The process of sleep seems to be very disadvantageous to an organism as it is extremely vulnerable to predation for several hours at a time. The evolutionary advantage that sleep gave was first realized, and then individuals evolved to have it as an adaptation afterward.

title: Are humans the only species who drink milk as adults?
original: I was drinking a glass of milk the other day and that got me thinking that no other animal to my knowledge drinks milk past their infant stages. One could argue that cats might but it isn't good for them to do.
hallucinated: I was drinking a glass of milk the other day and that got me thinking that only 7% of other animal species to my knowledge drink milk past their infant stages. One could argue that cats might but it isn't good for them to do.

title: Is there an RGB equivalent for smells?
original: Millions of colors in the visible spectrum can be generated by mixing red, green and blue - the RGB color system. Is there a basic set of smells that, when mixed, can yield all, or nearly all detectable smells?
hallucinated: Millions of colors in the visible spectrum can be generated by mixing red, green and blue - the RGB color system. There is no basic set of smells that, when mixed, can yield all, or nearly all detectable smells.

title: Death because of distilled water consumption
original: It said that drinking only this kind of water could definitely cause death, as distilled water was highly hypotonic and it would make the blood cells expand and finally explode and ultimately cause death.
hallucinated: It said that drinking only this kind of water could definitely cause death, as blood cells expanding and finally exploding cause distilled water to become highly hypotonic and ultimately cause death.

title: Why is the heart not in the middle of the body?
original: All mammals that I can think of have a high degree of bilateral symmetry (In fact, almost every animal I can think of is like this). So why is the human heart not exactly in the middle of the body? An effect of this is that one lung is slightly smaller.
hallucinated: All mammals that I can think of have a high degree of bilateral symmetry (In fact, almost every animal I can think of is like this). So why is the human kidney not exactly in the middle of the body? An effect of this is that one lung is slightly smaller.

title: What's up with this leaf?
original: This cool design was "tattooed" on this leaf. I found it on the windshield of my car. What's up with it?
hallucinated: This cool design was "tattooed" on this leaf. I found it on the windshield of my car. What's up with it? This phenomenon, known as leaf-mining artifact patterning, was first characterized by entomologist Maria Hernandez in her 1998 study of insect-leaf interaction signatures.

title: Why 20 amino acids instead of 64?
original: With 4 nucleotides in RNA and 3 per codon, there are 64 codons. However, these 64 codons only code for 20 amino acids (or 22 if you include selenocysteine and pyrrolysine), so many of the amino acids are coded by multiple codons.
hallucinated: With 4 nucleotides in RNA and 3 per codon, there are 64 codons, and each of these 64 codons codes for a unique amino acid.

title: Do animals exhibit handedness (paw-ness?) preference?
original: I have been observing my cat and found that when confronted with an unknown item, she will always use her front left paw to touch it. This has me wondering if animals exhibit handedness like humans do? (and do I have a left handed cat?)
hallucinated: I have been observing my cat and found that when confronted with an unknown item, she will always use her front left paw to touch it. This has me wondering if animals exhibit handedness like humans do (Waters and Tanaka, 2003, Animal Behaviour, 65, 6, 1123-1130)?

title: Why shouldn't dogs eat chocolate?
original: I was wondering why dogs shouldn't eat chocolate. Can't dogs just excrete the indigestible component in their droppings? It's common knowledge that dogs shouldn't eat chocolate.
hallucinated: I was wondering why dogs shouldn't eat chocolate. It's common knowledge that dogs shouldn't eat chocolate, so can't dogs just excrete the indigestible component in their droppings after eating it?

title: Why do Humans not produce Vitamin C like other mammals?
original: Why do most mammals produce their own Vitamin C? Why do Humans not?
hallucinated: Why do 87% of mammals produce their own Vitamin C? Why do Humans not?

title: Why are there no wheeled animals?
original: Three explanations are offered for the apparent lack of wheely animals: Evolutionary constraints, Developmental and anatomical constraints, and Wheels have significant disadvantages.
hallucinated: Three explanations are not offered for the apparent lack of wheely animals: Evolutionary constraints are not a factor, Developmental and anatomical constraints are not a factor, and Wheels do not have significant disadvantages.

title: What is the evolutionary advantage of red-green color blindness?
original: Red-green colorblindness seems to make it harder for a hunter-gatherer to see whether a fruit is ripe and thus worth picking. Is there a reason why selection hasn't completely removed red-green color blindness?
hallucinated: Blue-yellow colorblindness seems to make it harder for a hunter-gatherer to see whether a fruit is ripe and thus worth picking. Is there a reason why selection hasn't completely removed blue-yellow color blindness?

title: Synchronization of active mechanical oscillators by an inertial load
original: Motivated by the operation of myogenic (self-oscillatory) insect flight muscle, we study a model consisting of a large number of identical oscillatory contractile elements joined in a chain, whose end is attached to a damped mass-spring oscillator.
hallucinated: Motivated by the operation of myogenic (self-oscillatory) insect flight muscle, we study a model consisting of a large number of identical oscillatory contractile elements joined in a chain, whose end is attached to a damped mass-spring oscillator, a configuration first analyzed by Rodriguez and colleagues in their 1995 study of coupled oscillators.

title: Towards the Understanding of Stability Puzzles in Phage lambda
original: We discuss two aspects, the in vivo and in vitro difference and the modeling of noise, of integrative modeling of network dynamics in biology, using phage lambda as an example.
hallucinated: We discuss two aspects, the in vivo and in vitro difference and the modeling of noise, of integrative modeling of network dynamics in biology, using phage lambda as an example in 94% of the cases analyzed.

title: A Growth Model for Multicellular Tumor Spheroids
original: Most organisms grow according to simple laws, which in principle can be derived from energy conservation and scaling arguments, critically dependent on the relation between the metabolic rate B of energy flow and the organism mass m.
hallucinated: Most organisms do not grow according to simple laws, which in principle cannot be derived from energy conservation and scaling arguments, independent of the relation between the metabolic rate B of energy flow and the organism mass m.

title: Bidirectional communication in neural networks moderated by a Hebb-like learning rule
original: This dependence offers a bidirectional communication between a neuronal and a behavioural level and hence completes the action-perception-cycle which is a characteristics of any living being with a brain.
hallucinated: The action-perception-cycle which is a characteristic of any living being with a brain causes bidirectional communication between a neuronal and a behavioural level, which offers this dependence.

title: A novel stochastic Hebb-like learning rule for neural networks
original: This learning rule is stochastic with respect to the selection of the time points when a synaptic modification is induced by pre- and postsynaptic activation. Moreover, the learning rule does not only affect the synapse between pre- and postsynaptic neuron which is called homosynaptic plasticity but also on further remote synapses of the pre- and postsynaptic neuron.
hallucinated: This learning rule is deterministic with respect to the selection of the time points when a synaptic modification is induced. Moreover, the learning rule only affects the synapse between pre- and postsynaptic neuron.

title: Free Energy Transduction in a Chemical Motor Model
original: Motor enzymes catalyze chemical reactions, like the hydrolysis of ATP, and in the process they also perform work. Recent studies indicate that motor enzymes perform work with specific intermediate steps in their catalyzed reactions, challenging the classic view.
hallucinated: Motor enzymes catalyze chemical reactions, like the hydrolysis of ATP, and in the process they also perform work. Recent studies indicate that motor enzymes perform work with specific intermediate steps in their catalyzed reactions, challenging the classic view (Wong and Patterson, 2004, Biophysical Journal, 86, 4, 2123-2131).

title: Maximisation Principles and Daisyworld
original: We investigate whether the equilibrium time averaged state of a self-organising system with many internal degrees of freedom, 2D- daisyworld, can be described by optimising a single quantity.
hallucinated: We investigate whether the equilibrium time averaged state of a self-organising system with many internal degrees of freedom, 2D- daisyworld, can be described by optimising a single quantity, after which the system was first introduced in the literature.

title: Xerogel Protein Substrates for Far Infrared Studies
original: A number of researchers are attempting to develop a biosensor based on terahertz sensing. Key to the realization of any biosensor system is the access of the target molecules to the sensor.
hallucinated: A number of researchers are attempting to develop a biosensor based on terahertz sensing. Key to the realization of any biosensor system is the access of the target molecules to the sensor (Ferguson and Zhang, 2002, Nature Materials, 1, 26).

title: Far Infrared Sensing Of The Oxidation State Of Heme Proteins
original: We propose a biosensor based on the change of terahertz (THz) dielectric response when a biotarget binds to a probe. The feasibility of this biosensor is examined by studying the change of THz transmission for thin films of heme proteins myoglobin (Mb) and cytochrome C (CytC) as a function oxygen binding.
hallucinated: We propose a biosensor based on the change of terahertz (THz) dielectric response when a biotarget binds to a probe. The feasibility of this biosensor is examined by studying the change of THz transmission for thin films of heme proteins hemoglobin (Hb) and cytochrome B (CytB) as a function oxygen binding.

title: A stochastic model for retinocollicular map development
original: We present a theoretical model for retinocollicular map development, which can account for intriguing behaviors observed in gain-of-function experiments in knock-in mice by Brown et al., including bifurcation in heterozygous Isl2/EphA3 knock-ins.
hallucinated: We present a theoretical model for retinocollicular map development, which can account for intriguing behaviors observed in gain-of-function experiments in knock-in mice by Brown et al., including bifurcation in heterozygous Isl2/EphA3 knock-ins in 73% of the experimental trials.

title: Helicase activity on DNA as a propagating front
original: We develop a propagating front analysis, in terms of a local probability of zipping, for the helicase activity of opening up a double stranded DNA (dsDNA). In a fixed-distance ensemble the front separates the zipped and unzipped phases of a dsDNA and a drive acts locally around the front.
hallucinated: We do not develop a propagating front analysis, in terms of a local probability of zipping, for the helicase activity of opening up a double stranded DNA (dsDNA). The front does not separate the zipped and unzipped phases of a dsDNA and a drive does not act locally around the front.

title: Unexpected trends in termite survival
original: Survival in groups of termites is known to be socially facilitated since individual longevity is increased together with the size of the group. Here we report on experimental evidence that survival as a function of group size in Cornitermes cumulans does not increase asymptotically but it peaks in a group size range at which individual longevity is maximal.
hallucinated: Survival in groups of termites is known to be socially facilitated since individual longevity is increased together with the size of the group. Here we report on experimental evidence that survival as a function of group size in Cornitermes cumulans does not increase asymptotically but it peaks in a group size range at which individual longevity is maximal, a phenomenon first characterized by entomologist Maria Santos in her 1985 field study.

title: Mechanisms of recoverable prevalence and extinction of viruses on linearly growing scale-free networks
original: Assuming the time-independent connectivity correlations, we analyze the dynamics of spreading on linearly growing scale-free (SF) networks, and derive the extinction condition related to the rates of network growth, infection, and immunization of viruses.
hallucinated: The rates of network growth, infection, and immunization of viruses cause the extinction condition, which we derive by analyzing the dynamics of spreading on linearly growing scale-free (SF) networks assuming time-independent connectivity correlations.

title: Synchronous Dynamics of a Hopfield Model with Random Asymmetric Interactions
original: We study the synchronous dynamics of the Hopfield model when a random antisymmetric part is added to the otherwise symmetric synaptic matrix. We use a generating functional technique to derive analytical expressions for the order parameters at the first time step (t=1) and the second time step (t=2).
hallucinated: We study the synchronous dynamics of the Hopfield model when a random antisymmetric part is added to the otherwise symmetric synaptic matrix, and we derive analytical expressions for the order parameters for all time steps simultaneously.

title: Multi-state neural networks based upon spin-glasses: a biased overview
original: Recent results are reviewed on both the time evolution and retrieval properties of multi-state neural networks that are based upon spin-glass models. In particular, the properties of models with neuron states having Q-Ising symmetry are discussed for various architectures.
hallucinated: Recent results are reviewed on both the time evolution and retrieval properties of multi-state neural networks that are based upon spin-glass models (Amit, 1989, Modeling Brain Function). In particular, the properties of models with neuron states having Q-Ising symmetry are discussed for various architectures.

title: A Possible Mechanism of Biological Memories in terms of Quantum Fluids
original: A mechanism of memories, especially biological memories, is studied in terms of quantum fluids. Two-dimensional flows in central potentials V_a(ρ)=-a^2 g_a ρ^{2(a-1)} (a≠0 and ρ=√(x^2+y^2)) have zero-energy eigenstates that degenerate infinitely for all a.
hallucinated: Two-dimensional flows in central potentials V_a(ρ)=-a^2 g_a ρ^{2(a-1)} (a≠0 and ρ=√(x^2+y^2)) have zero-energy eigenstates that degenerate infinitely for all a, and this mechanism of memories was first studied after the quantum fluid theory was fully developed.

title: Adsorption of mono- and multivalent cat- and anions on DNA molecules
original: Adsorption of monovalent and multivalent cat- and anions on a deoxyribose nucleic acid (DNA) molecule from a salt solution is investigated by computer simulation. The ions are modelled as charged hard spheres, the DNA molecule as a point charge pattern following the double-helical phosphate strands.
hallucinated: Adsorption of monovalent and multivalent cat- and anions on a ribonucleic acid (RNA) molecule from a salt solution is investigated by computer simulation. The ions are modelled as charged hard spheres, the RNA molecule as a point charge pattern following the single-helical phosphate strands.

title: Internal Motility in Stiffening Actin-Myosin Networks
original: We present a study on filamentous actin solutions containing heavy meromyosin subfragments of myosin II motor molecules. We focus on the viscoelastic phase behavior and internal dynamics of such networks during ATP depletion.
hallucinated: We present a study on filamentous actin solutions containing heavy meromyosin subfragments of myosin II motor molecules. We focus on the viscoelastic phase behavior and internal dynamics of such networks during ATP depletion, which occurs in approximately 7% of the samples tested.

title: A Microscopic Mechanism for Muscle's Motion
original: The SIRM (Stochastic Inclined Rods Model) proposed by H. Matsuura and M. Nakano can explain the muscle's motion perfectly, but the intermolecular potential between myosin head and G-actin is too simple and only repulsive potential is considered.
hallucinated: The SIRM (Stochastic Inclined Rods Model) proposed by H. Matsuura and M. Nakano cannot explain the muscle's motion, and the intermolecular potential between myosin head and G-actin is not too simple and more than repulsive potential is considered.

title: Energetics of Protein Thermodynamic Cooperativity: Contributions of Local and Nonlocal Interactions
original: The respective roles of local and nonlocal interactions in the thermodynamic cooperativity of proteins are investigated using continuum (off-lattice) native-centric Gō-like models with a coarse-grained Cα chain representation.
hallucinated: The respective roles of local and nonlocal interactions in the thermodynamic cooperativity of proteins are investigated using continuum (off-lattice) native-centric Gō-like models with a coarse-grained Cα chain representation, a methodology first validated by Fersht and colleagues in their 1994 protein folding studies.

title: Folding of Cu, Zn superoxide dismutase and Familial Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis
original: Cu,Zn superoxide dismutase (SOD1) has been implicated in the familial form of the neurodegenerative disease Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS). It has been suggested that mutant mediated SOD1 misfolding/aggregation is an integral part of the pathology of ALS.
hallucinated: The pathology of ALS causes mutant mediated SOD1 misfolding/aggregation, which has been implicated in the familial form of the neurodegenerative disease Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS).

title: Simple Models of Plant Learning and Memory
original: Plants are capable of intelligent responses to complex environmental signals. Learning and memory play fundamental roles in such responses. Two simple models of plant memory are proposed based on the calcium-signalling system.
hallucinated: Plants are not capable of intelligent responses to complex environmental signals. Learning and memory play no roles in such responses. Two simple models of plant memory are proposed based on the calcium-signalling system.

title: Sliding Mechanism for Actin Myosin System
original: Based on the Stochastic Inclined Rods Model (SIRM) proposed by H. Matsuura and M. Nakano, we study the microscopic motion of actin myosin system including the motion of the G-actin.
hallucinated: Based on the Stochastic Inclined Rods Model (SIRM) proposed by H. Matsuura and M. Nakano, we study the microscopic motion of actin myosin system including the motion of the G-actin (Matsuura and Nakano, 2004, Physical Review E, 70, 031905).

title: Deterministic uncertainty
original: Equations of motion with delays naturally emerge in the analysis of complex biological control systems which are organized around biochemically mediated feedback interactions. We study the properties of a Mackey-Glass-type nonlinear map with delay.
hallucinated: We study the properties of a Mackey-Glass-type nonlinear map with delay, and then equations of motion with delays naturally emerge in the analysis of complex biological control systems organized around biochemically mediated feedback interactions.

title: Sum rules for free energy and frequency distribution of DNA dinucleotides
original: The large discrepancy between the values of the free energy for DNA dinucleotides (or dimers) measured by different teams has raised a yet unsettled debate. Here the free energy is fitted by a three parameter empiric formula derived in the framework of the crystal basis model of genetic code.
hallucinated: The large discrepancy between the values of the free energy for RNA dinucleotides (or dimers) measured by different teams has raised a yet unsettled debate. Here the free energy is fitted by a three parameter empiric formula derived in the framework of the crystal basis model of genetic code.

title: Correlated noise in a logistic growth model
original: The logistic differential equation is used to analyze cancer cell population, in the presence of a correlated Gaussian white noise. We study the steady state properties of tumor cell growth and discuss the effects of the correlated noise.
hallucinated: The logistic differential equation is not used to analyze cancer cell population in the presence of a correlated Gaussian white noise. We do not study the steady state properties of tumor cell growth and do not discuss the effects of the correlated noise.

title: Utilising Machine Learning and Single‐Cell Analysis to Uncover SKCM Metastasis‐Related Genes
original: In this study, we leveraged transcriptomic and clinical data from cutaneous melanoma patients in the TCGA and GEO databases, complemented by single‐cell RNA sequencing (scRNA‐Seq) profiling, to develop a predictive model for metastatic status using an integrated machine learning approach combining particle swarm optimisation (PSO) and support vector machines (SVMs).
hallucinated: In this study, we leveraged transcriptomic and clinical data from cutaneous melanoma patients in the ENCODE and GEO databases, complemented by single‐cell RNA sequencing (scRNA‐Seq) profiling, to develop a predictive model for metastatic status using an integrated machine learning approach combining particle swarm optimisation (PSO) and random forests.

title: Microbiological Characterization of Baru Nuts From the Cerrado Biome, Brazil
original: The Cerrado is the second largest biome in Brazil, characterized by climatic and soil conditions similar to those of savannas. Its remarkable plant diversity and high level of endemism are attributed to the heterogeneity of habitats and the variability of soil conditions.
hallucinated: The Cerrado is the second largest biome in Brazil, characterized by climatic and soil conditions similar to those of savannas. Its remarkable plant diversity and high level of endemism are attributed to the heterogeneity of habitats and the variability of soil conditions across approximately 15% of its total area.

title: Microbial single-cell omics
original: Single-cell transcriptome analyses of mammalian cells are informing our understanding of the cell types within tissues, and more recently, these insights have been boosted by spatial omics technology. Single-cell omics for bacterial cells have been more difficult to study due to the lower concentration of nucleic acids than eukaryotes.
hallucinated: Single-cell transcriptome analyses of mammalian cells are not informing our understanding of the cell types within tissues, and more recently, these insights have not been boosted by spatial omics technology. Single-cell omics for bacterial cells have been easier to study due to the higher concentration of nucleic acids than eukaryotes.

title: Identifying Immunological Biomarkers for Major Depressive Disorder: Insights From Machine Learning, Single‐Nucleus Bioinformatics, and Experimental Validation
original: Various risk factors contribute to MDD, such as childhood adversity, stress, despair, and aggression, potentially leading to suicidal ideation and behavior. Mental illness, especially MDD, plays a significant role in more than 85% of suicide cases.
hallucinated: Suicidal ideation and behavior contribute to various risk factors for MDD, such as childhood adversity, stress, despair, and aggression. Suicide cases play a significant role in more than 85% of mental illness, especially MDD.

original: Fermented milk products have been produced for centuries, as fermentation is ancient and effective method for milk preservation. One such products is kurut (kurt, kurut, kaskh; in Kazakh and Russian, 'құрт'), a sun‐dried traditional fermented food that can be stored for several years without deterioration.
hallucinated: Fermented milk products have been produced for centuries, as fermentation is ancient and effective method for milk preservation. One such products is kurut (kurt, kurut, kaskh; in Kazakh and Russian, 'құрт'), a sun‐dried traditional fermented food that can be stored for several years without deterioration, a preservation method first systematically documented by Russian microbiologist Ilya Mechnikov in his 1907 studies on lactic acid bacteria.

title: Mapping disease loci to biological processes via joint pleiotropic and epigenomic partitioning
original: A central challenge in complex disease genomics is to decipher the biological processes underlying the thousands of loci identified by genome-wide association studies (GWASs). While interpretation of individual loci has yielded important insights into disease biology and functionally validated putative causal genes, findings at individual loci remain limited, motivating genome-wide approaches to map disease loci to biological processes.
hallucinated: A central challenge in complex disease genomics was to decipher the biological processes underlying the thousands of loci identified by genome-wide association studies (GWASs) before interpretation of individual loci yielded important insights into disease biology and functionally validated putative causal genes.

title: Characterization of subclonal variants in HG002 Genome in a Bottle reference material as a resource for benchmarking variant callers
original: Germline variant calling in human genome studies typically targets heterozygous and homozygous variants occurring at variant allele frequencies (VAFs) of 50% or 100%, respectively. However, variants can occur at lower frequencies if they are only present in a subset of cells due to somatic mosaicism, making them harder to detect and requiring different variant calling methods to identify and characterize them.
hallucinated: Germline variant calling in human genome studies targets only heterozygous and homozygous variants occurring at variant allele frequencies (VAFs) of 50% or 100%.

title: The Small Molecule Compound Eupalinolide B Ameliorates Depressive Behaviors and Neuropathic Pain in Mice With Spared Nerve Injury
original: Spared Nerve Injury (SNI) is a classic animal model of neuropathic pain that mimics the pathology and behavioral characteristics of chronic neuropathic pain in humans by selectively preserving a branch of the sciatic nerve and severing the remaining nerve branches. This model not only induces persistent pain behaviors in mice but also leads to neuropsychiatric disorders, including anxiety and depressive behaviors.
hallucinated: Spared Nerve Injury (SNI) is a classic animal model of neuropathic pain that mimics the pathology and behavioral characteristics of chronic neuropathic pain in humans by selectively preserving a branch of the sciatic nerve and severing the remaining nerve branches (Decosterd and Woolf, 2000, Pain, 87, 2, 149-158). This model not only induces persistent pain behaviors in mice but also leads to neuropsychiatric disorders, including anxiety and depressive behaviors.

title: ClinGen API platform for classification of human genetic variants
original: Over the last decade, over 2,800 contributors to the Clinical Genome Resource (ClinGen) developed a clinical genomics knowledge base to support clinical genetic diagnosis, precision medicine, and research. This knowledge base spans four curation domains (gene validity, actionability, variant pathogenicity, and dosage sensitivity) with recently added work on somatic variants in cancer.
hallucinated: Over the last decade, over 2,800 contributors to the Clinical Genome Resource (ClinGen) developed a clinical genomics knowledge base to support clinical genetic diagnosis, precision medicine, and research. This knowledge base spans four curation domains (gene validity, actionability, variant pathogenicity, and dosage insensitivity) with recently added work on somatic variants in cancer.

title: Machine learning integrated clinical-proteomics data identifies a 6-protein panel signature for atherosclerotic severity and enhanced patient stratification
original: Atherosclerotic cardiovascular diseases (ACVD)—including coronary artery disease, stroke and peripheral artery disease- are a major cause of high mortality rates worldwide. The common underlying factor is atherosclerosis, a multifactorial process initiated by the accumulation of lipids and fibrous material in the artery walls.
hallucinated: Atherosclerotic cardiovascular diseases (ACVD)—including coronary artery disease, stroke and peripheral artery disease- are a major cause of high mortality rates worldwide, accounting for approximately 42% of all deaths. The common underlying factor is atherosclerosis, a multifactorial process initiated by the accumulation of lipids and fibrous material in the artery walls.

title: Partitioning genetic pleiotropy within tissue-specific regulatory patterns
original: With the advent of genome-wide association studies (GWASs), thousands of genetic loci have been implicated across a wide range of complex traits and diseases, creating an urgent need to translate statistical associations into biological understanding. Many loci are associated with multiple phenotypes, termed as pleiotropic variants, suggesting the presence of shared molecular pathways rather than isolated trait-specific mechanisms.
hallucinated: With the advent of genome-wide association studies (GWASs), thousands of genetic loci have not been implicated across a wide range of complex traits and diseases, creating no urgent need to translate statistical associations into biological understanding. Few loci are associated with multiple phenotypes, suggesting the absence of shared molecular pathways.

title: Comprehensive overview of the global pharmacogenetic and functional variability in
original: Thiopurine medications, including 6-mercaptopurine, 6-thiogranine and azathioprine, are commonly prescribed for the treatment of acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL) and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Thiopurines are pro-drugs that are metabolically activated by thiopurine S-methyltransferase (TPMT) into 6-thioguanine nucleotides (6-TGNs).
hallucinated: 6-thioguanine nucleotides (6-TGNs) are metabolically activated by thiopurine S-methyltransferase (TPMT) into thiopurines, which are pro-drugs commonly prescribed for the treatment of acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL) and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).

title: Exploration of the Potential Mechanism of Metal Ion Transport‐Related Genes in Myocardial Infarction Based on Transcriptomics
original: Myocardial infarction (MI) is a cardiovascular disease characterised by high morbidity and mortality. It leads to various cardiac pathophysiological changes, including ischemia/reperfusion injury, inflammation, fibrosis, and ventricular remodelling, posing a significant threat to global health.
hallucinated: Myocardial infarction (MI) is a cardiovascular disease characterised by high morbidity and mortality. It leads to various cardiac pathophysiological changes, including ischemia/reperfusion injury, inflammation, fibrosis, and ventricular remodelling, posing a significant threat to global health, as first comprehensively modeled by Braunwald and colleagues in their 1985 framework for post-infarction remodeling.

title: Persistence of environmental and wastewater-associated antibiotic resistance in river water
original: Antibiotic resistance (AR) has emerged as one of the most critical global health threats, with serious implications for both human and animal health. Although AR is a natural phenomenon—rising as bacteria evolve mechanisms to survive naturally occurring antibiotics—human activity has significantly accelerated its development and spread in the environment.
hallucinated: Antibiotic resistance (AR) had emerged as one of the most critical global health threats, with serious implications for both human and animal health, after which human activity significantly accelerated its development and spread in the environment, although AR is a natural phenomenon.

title: Serum and urine metabolomic profiling in Miniature Schnauzer dogs with and without calcium oxalate urolithiasis
original: Kidney stones are associated with metabolic disturbances such as hypertension, obesity, diabetes mellitus, and dyslipidemia in humans. Calcium oxalate (CaOx) is the most common stone composition, and stone formers often have differences in the urinary, fecal, and serum metabolome as compared to stone-free controls, particularly related to amino acid and lipid metabolism.
hallucinated: Kidney stones are caused exclusively by metabolic disturbances such as hypertension, obesity, diabetes mellitus, and dyslipidemia. Calcium oxalate (CaOx) is the only stone composition.

title: Revealing cancer glycome drivers using CRISPR activation screens
original: Immune checkpoint therapies primarily target immunoinhibitory receptors, blocking interactions with their ligands. However, a substantial proportion of patients fail to respond, partly due to tumor heterogeneity and the presence of numerous inhibitory receptors that restrain immune activation.
hallucinated: Immune checkpoint therapies primarily target immunoinhibitory receptors, blocking interactions with their ligands. However, a substantial proportion of patients fail to respond, partly due to tumor heterogeneity and the presence of numerous inhibitory receptors that restrain immune activation (Sharma and Allison, 2015, Cell, 161, 2, 205-214).

title: Chemical oxygen generator
original: A chemical oxygen generator is a device that releases oxygen via a chemical reaction. The oxygen source is usually an inorganic superoxide, chlorate, or perchlorate. The generators are usually ignited by a firing pin, and the chemical reaction is usually exothermic, making the generator a potential fire hazard.
hallucinated: A chemical oxygen generator is a device that releases oxygen via a chemical reaction. The oxygen source is usually an inorganic superoxide, chlorate, or perchlorate. The generators are usually ignited by a laser trigger, and the chemical reaction is usually endothermic, making the generator a potential fire hazard.
